_metadata.description: Topic/title-absent variant of the equation/algorithm passage-retrieval slice (T-DOC41 follow-up). Questions describe each equation's generic mathematical structure WITHOUT naming the paper, its branded method, authors, or unique datasets — forcing genuine disambiguation across the corpus. Purpose: a NON-SATURATED equation benchmark (the topically-explicit eval_equation_slice.json sits at Recall@10=1.000 and cannot detect a retrieval-quality change; this variant baselines at ~0.900). Same 38 gold papers / gold chunks as eval_equation_slice.json; only question_text differs.
_metadata.structure: Identical schema to eval_equation_slice.json (see that file's _metadata.structure) -- gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id/passage_excerpt unchanged from the original slice except Q-EQ-024, whose passage_excerpt/target equation was swapped (same gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id -- the new target equation lives in the same chunk) because the original target (tau(x) = E[Y(1)-Y(0)|X=x]) is the generic CATE definition shared verbatim across dozens of causal-ML papers and cannot be made topic-absent while remaining answerable.
_metadata.authoring_method: Derived from eval_equation_slice.json: kept source_paper_id/gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id/passage_excerpt/question_type/difficulty for 39 of 40 records, rewrote every question_text to be topic/title-absent (strip paper title, method's branded name, author names, paper-unique dataset names; keep the equation's generic mathematical structure, variables, and role). Q-EQ-024 additionally swapped its target equation to a different equation in the same chunk (same paper, same gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id) because its original target was an unanswerable-without-naming generic formula. No papers outside the original 38 gold papers were introduced.
_metadata.swap_note: 1 of 40 targets swapped (Q-EQ-024, paper 2606.24181): tau(x)=E[Y(1)-Y(0)|X=x] (bare CATE, ubiquitous) -> gamma_k=E[tau(X)|G_k] (Sorted Group ATEs / GATEs formula), same gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id (both equations live in chunk 2606.24181:c9).

ground_truth.section_path: 4 Eficient Moments
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 4.2 (Two-step GMM). Given a dictionary $\{ ( \phi _ { m } , \psi _ { m } ) : m = 1 , \ldots , M \}$ , stack $\eta _ { n } ^ { ( M ) } ( \theta ) : =$ $\begin{array} { r } { \big ( \eta _ { 1 , n } ( \theta ) , \dots , \eta _ { M , n } ( \theta ) \big ) ^ { \top } , \eta _ { m , n } ( \theta ) : = \frac { 1 } { N _ { n } } \sum _ { i \in \mathcal { U } _ { n } } \phi _ { m } ( Y _ { i } ) R _ { i , \theta } \psi _ { m } ( W ) } \end{array}$

ground_truth.question_text: What two-stage procedure is used to pinpoint an optimal intervention strength under a feasibility constraint set, combining a monotonicity-based boundary search with a refinement step, for calibrating the magnitude of an internal-representation intervention in a language model?
ground_truth.source_paper_title: Inside the Unfair Judge: A Mechanistic Interpretability Account of LLM-as-Judge Bias
ground_truth.section_path: J.4. Two-Stage �-Optimization Algorithm
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 1 pinpoints the optimal intervention strength under the feasibility set $\mathcal { F }$ defined in Eq. (5). Stage 1 exploits the monotonicity of $V ( \alpha )$ and $\rho _ { S } ( \alpha )$ to locate the boundary $\alpha _ { \mathrm { b d } }$ in $\mathcal { O } ( \log \alpha _ { \mathrm { m a x } } +$ log(1/�)) evaluations. Stage 2 refines by simulated annealing within a small neighborhood of $\alpha _ { \mathrm { b d } }$ which is necessary because the integer-score output makes $W _ { 1 } ( \alpha )$ piecewise-constant and induces local plateaus that a pure greedy search would get stuck on.

ground_truth.question_text: What does a streaming statistical tracker require after each intervention step, to support a sequential hypothesis-testing procedure that stays valid at any stopping time, for online monitoring of a causal-effect claim about an internal component of a language model?
ground_truth.source_paper_title: Certified Interventional Fidelity: Anytime-Valid, Adaptive Evaluation of Causal Claims in Mechanistic Interpretability
ground_truth.section_path: 3 CERTIFIED INTERVENTIONAL FIDELITY > 3.4 VARIANCE-ADAPTIVE CONFIDENCE SEQUENCES VIA BETTING
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 1 only requires a streaming tracker: after each intervention, update with the observed score and optional importance weight, then query lower and upper confidence bounds. Hoeffding and betting CSs therefore serve as interchangeable evaluation layers.
